text: Get closer for a good look.
speaker: narrator

text: The remaining pages are blank, awaiting further tales of adventure.
speaker: narrator

text: The writing on this page looks very recent.  It says "I, %s, have come to Spielburg to become a Hero."
speaker: narrator
why: contains printf-style substitution code

text: The %s entry was made several years ago.  It says: "Baronet Barnard von Spielburg killed a Troll near the Flying Falls on this 23 day of Octember".
speaker: narrator
why: contains printf-style substitution code

text: The other pages record the exploits of the great adventurers of Spielburg's past.  The names Wolfgang Abenteuer and Baron Stefan von Spielburg appear most often.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like this old Guild Master doesn't do too much adventuring any more.  Still, he looks like he was plenty tough in his day, and he probably has many a tale to tell.
speaker: narrator

text: The fire blazing cheerily in the fireplace helps warm the Guild Master's old bones.
speaker: narrator

text: The plaque under the Moose says: "Courtesy of Sierra Online Prop Dept."
speaker: narrator

text: You never saw a purple Saurus before you came to Spielburg, but it looks like a really stupid monster.  The plaque reads:  "Saurus slain by Hans Halfwitten".
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like it must have been a particularly nasty Troll.  You wouldn't want to meet him in a dark forest.  The plaque reads:  "Troll slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
speaker: narrator

text: This crossbreed of eagle and lion could have torn a man apart when it was alive.  The plaque reads: "Gryphon slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
speaker: narrator

text: Even in death, this monster remains awesome.  The plaque reads: "Dragon slain by Baron Stefan von Spielburg".
speaker: narrator

text: The head is like a panther's, but with a strong human-like quality.  It is still rather frightening.  The plaque reads:  "Cheetaur slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
speaker: narrator

text: This is certainly a weird one! You've never seen anything quite like it.  The plaque reads: "Antwerp slain by Two Guys From Andromeda".
speaker: narrator

text: This Adventurer's Guild Hall reminds you of the one in your home town.  The traditional Moose head and other stuffed monsters (Saurus, Troll, Gryphon, Dragon, Cheetaur, and the terrible Antwerp) adorn the walls.
speaker: narrator

text: You see the registration book on the desk and the bulletin board full of job listings.  The man seated by the fire must be the Guild Master.  He is snoring.
speaker: narrator

text: There is nothing here that you need, and the Guild Master may be more aware than he seems.
speaker: narrator

text: The desk drawers are empty.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no way to close the desk.
speaker: narrator

text: On the desk are an old but ornate leather-bound book and a quill pen in the ink bottle.
speaker: narrator

text: You see an ordinary pen and ink.
speaker: narrator

text: On the walls hang dusty, moth-eaten monster heads.  You recognize the purple Saurus, the Troll, the Gryphon, and the Dragon.
speaker: narrator

text: You figure that the black head must belong to the Cheetaur, and you have no idea what the four-eyed creature might be.  You are also not quite sure why the Moose head is considered traditional, but every Guild Hall seems to have one.
speaker: narrator

text: The desk holds a pen, an inkwell, and the Adventurer's Guild Registration and Log Book.
speaker: narrator

text: But you've already done that!
speaker: narrator

text: You sign your name into the Adventurer's Log Book with a flourish.
speaker: narrator

text: He already is.
speaker: narrator

text: "Oh, my young friend!  What shall we talk about?  I have so many stories.  Just ask me about something."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Ja.  What with the baron losing his son and daughter, all the monsters, Baba Yaga, and now the brigands, it has been one thing after another."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when we were younger.  No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said that he goes nowhere and sees no one."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "He was a hero and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg.  He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago, and his horse returned with large and deep claw marks.  No sign of the Baronet's body was ever found."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Elsa was a beautiful eight-year-old child with blonde hair and sky-blue eyes.  She was the Baron's pride and joy."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Ten years ago she was carried off by something which came over the wall and flew off with her.  The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Everyone, that is, except for the Baron's Jester, Yorick."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogress.  Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Did I ever tell you about the days when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a true Guild Hall.  Now we are just old men, and this is just a place to tell old stories."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Schultz and I have been friends for a long time.  He too has been a real adventurer.  Now, he is just the Sheriff of the town."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Even a thief can be a hero sometimes, as long as uses his skills only for good purposes.  But most thieves are far from being heroes."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "This is, of course, where an adventurer can find out where there is a need for someone brave and courageous.  There are jobs on the bulletin board over there."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "It is also a good place to talk about adventures on a cold afternoon.  We used to play cards here once a week, as well, but there are too few adventurers in Spielburg anymore.  They all died at the hands of monsters or brigands, or they just became too old."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "You can see some of the types of monsters that live around here if you look at our walls."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "There is a reward for anyone who can stop the brigands by capturing or killing their leaders.  The information is on the bulletin board."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Oh, those are nothing.  Anyone can kill a Saurus."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "It's been a while since Schultz and I killed the one on the wall.  It is fortunate that few Trolls remain; they're deadly."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Watch out for Cheetaurs.  I bear the scars of my last fight with one to this day."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "I remember the day I killed one.  It was the biggest one I had ever seen.  It put up a tough fight, but I was tougher.  You can see the result on the wall."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "One day, years ago, a pair of dragons tried to take over our valley.  We adventurers rode out to meet them."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "I can still see Stefan von Spielburg charging forward on his black horse.  He slew the one whose head adorns the wall above our fireplace.  The other one flew off."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "People say that they can sometimes see that other dragon flying high overhead, but it has never dared attack us again."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "One year, this valley was overrun by those odd and terrible monsters.  Schultz and I fought long and hard to eliminate them completely.  We might have failed even so, had it not been for those two peculiar tourists who came to our aid."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "That was the most vicious moose I ever ran into.  Nearly bit my nose off!"
speaker: wolfgang

text: "They certainly were strange."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "That's where the tourists said they came from.  Must be some place up North -- I sure never heard of it."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Not much of an adventurer, but he did kill one Saurus before he made the mistake of tangling with an Ogre."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Ogres are a lot like Goons, but even meaner.  Not as bright, though."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "A Goon is a lot like an Ogre, not as tough, but a little smarter.  We have two Goons in town, Otto and Crusher."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "I am Wolfgang Abenteuer, Guild Master of this hall."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "A real hero is someone who did not start out strong or powerful, but who uses his courage, brains, and skills to become the best he can be."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "I'm sorry.  I only know what's written on the poster."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "The castle is just north of the Healer's house."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "The Healer's house is just north of the crossroads outside of town."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "You know, that reminds me very little of the time old Schultz and I......."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Wait a minute. It'll come to me."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ."
speaker: wolfgang

text: "Ach! I was so busy I didn't notice you come in. Welcome, welcome! It is so seldom that we have new adventurers here. Most people think this valley is cursed!"
speaker: wolfgang

text: The breeze is cool, but you feel a shiver deeper than just the cold.  You are on your own in a very dangerous place.

text: "For another silver, I can tell ya about where to go to find the Baron, Baba Yaga, or some goblins who have a lot of treasure. So if ya want some information, hand me some cash."
speaker: bruno

text: You don't have enough money.

text: "I seen her one time yelling out to the house, 'Hut of brown, now sit down.'  That's one spooky dame, let me tell you."
speaker: bruno

text: "For ten golds, I'll tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley."
speaker: bruno

text: "They hide it in the graveyard.  It's under one of the tombstones.  You can see the thieves if you go there at night."
speaker: bruno

text: "Okay, whaddaya wanna know?"
speaker: bruno

text: "For ten golds, I'll even tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley."
speaker: bruno

text: "Go south and you'll see a hollow log.  Then head west until ya can go south again.  Then go east until you can go south again.  From there, go west.  Them brigands just love visitors."
speaker: bruno

text: "For two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into old Baba Yaga's hut."
speaker: bruno

text: "Nobody knows much about the leader."
speaker: bruno

text: "For ten gold, I'll tell ya where some of those robbers are that're robbin' folks as they come inta the valley."
speaker: bruno

text: "Just call me Bruno.  That was an easy silver."
speaker: bruno

text: "She's an ugly hag who knows some magic.  She lives in a weird house."
speaker: bruno

text: "Her place is due west of the Baron's place.  Can't miss it.  And for two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into her hut."
speaker: bruno

text: "Ya just take the road north from here past the Healer's and go till ya get to his castle."
speaker: bruno

text: "The little pests are just northwest of the cemetery.  They're a bunch of wimps."
speaker: bruno

text: "It's due west of town.  Nice place to visit, but I wouldn't want to stay, if ya know what I mean."
speaker: bruno

text: "Everyone loves Dragon's Breath."
speaker: bruno

text: "The Sheriff and the goon are pals.   Otto even lives with the Sheriff and his wife."
speaker: bruno

text: "He's a pal of mine.  He usually hangs out in the bar.  A real sweet guy.  Give him a slap on the back for me."
speaker: bruno

text: "The thieves' password?  Sorry, I can't risk losing my thief license.  Chief wouldn't like it if I blabbed that to the likes'a you."
speaker: bruno

text: "He's the only one in the valley who can beat me at daggers."
speaker: bruno

text: "She's got some good potions.  Her hut is just to the north of here."
speaker: bruno

text: "He's some kinda magic user.  And he laughs alot.  That's all I know."
speaker: bruno

text: "Hahahaha!  Yeh, the antwerp is a good monster for you to fight.  It loves to play."
speaker: bruno

text: "Lots'a monsters in this valley.  Most of 'em are wimps, though."
speaker: bruno

text: "Hey, I just know what I know.  If you want other info, ask someone else."
speaker: bruno

text: "For a gold, well, I can tell where the Thieves' Guild is hidden."

text: "What's to tell?  You've seen her for yourself."
speaker: bonehead

text: "Baba Yaga is the most powerful Ogress around.  If you have any brains (and it looks like you don't), you'll stay away from her."
speaker: bonehead

text: "Some hero YOU are!  Don't know what an Ogre is.  Just check out Baba Yaga!"
speaker: bonehead

text: "All I ask for is a little gem or jewel that glows in the dark.   You're the big hero, so go find one."

text: "I wish I had some."

text: "I'm thrilled to have some!"

speaker: bonehead

text: "It's not much, but I call it home."
speaker: bonehead

text: "You've seen one, you've seen them all, I say."

text: "Of course, most fences aren't poisoned on top!"

text: "What an awful creature, to turn me into a FROG!", you say to yourself.  "Someday, I must return the favor."
speaker: hero

text: "I am sorry, but I'm much too broken to talk about such things right now.  I have lost everything!"
speaker: abdulla

text: The merchant consumes your meal in record time.
speaker: narrator

text: He then procedes to finish your drink in one gulp.
speaker: narrator

text: The merchant gratefully accepts your food ration, and consumes it in record time.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't give what you don't have.
speaker: narrator

text: The merchant finishes your drink in one gulp.
speaker: narrator

text: It is the merchant's hat.
speaker: narrator

text: It is the merchant's turban.
speaker: narrator

text: "There were about twelve brigands, including a Minotaur, if you can believe it!  Their leader was wearing a hooded cloak.  I could not see the face, but he had a high-pitched voice.  There was also some sort of warlock who giggled a lot."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Although worn with age, the brigand leader's cloak must have, at one time, been an exquisite garment.  No doubt it was stolen, as were all of my goods!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Perhaps it was my eyes that deceived me, but I could swear there was Gnome blood in that odd magician."
speaker: abdulla

text: "The magician who travels with the brigands is no wizard."
speaker: abdulla

text: "It is my hat."
speaker: abdulla

text: "It is my turban."
speaker: abdulla

text: "I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and formerly Master Merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Oh, I cannot bear to talk about my family at this sad time!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "You must not have heard of the brigands who prey upon the innocent and unwary."
speaker: abdulla

text: "All that I once owned is gone, alas!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Who would have thought that there could be such trouble entering this valley during the off-season?"
speaker: abdulla

text: "My good friends Shema and Shameen, owners of this inn, are the finest of all the Katta people that ever graced Shapeir!  But for their kindness, I would have starved long since."
speaker: abdulla

text: "If you have never seen such a beast before, you may count yourself lucky.  It is like a giant of a man with a bull's head."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Two weeks ago, I was about to become wealthy beyond my dreams.  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into the valley this year.  But my life was shattered by those vile brigands!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. They first used some magic which blinded us.  Then they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants."
speaker: abdulla

text: "All of my trade goods were taken from me, right before my very eyes!  Why the brigand leader spared my life, I do not know.  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Ah, Shapeir!  Beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun! The heart of civilization!  Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinni and Efreets, who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land."
speaker: abdulla

text: "But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Alas, had I known magic, perhaps I could have turned the tide against those abominable brigands.  Their magic did not seem so strong, but they outnumbered my poor caravan by two to one!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "My guards are all gone, killed or run away like dogs!  I, too, would be lying dead had not the strange brigand leader spared my life, for what reason I know not!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero."
speaker: abdulla

text: "Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it."
speaker: abdulla

text: "If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heroes, too!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!"
speaker: abdulla

text: "Hello again, my friend."
speaker: abdulla

text: Looking at the Centaur's muscles, his hoe, and his four gleaming hooves, you get the feeling that hostility is not the best of ideas.
speaker: narrator

text: The farmer has painstakingly removed all the rocks from his fields.
speaker: narrator

text: Don't throw things here.  The farmer wouldn't appreciate it.
speaker: narrator

text: The majestic Centaur watches you carefully.
speaker: narrator

text: The handsome Centaur has a look of pride and dignity as he rakes his field.
speaker: narrator

text: There's a world of difference between a horse and a Centaur!
speaker: narrator

text: The Centaur is half man, half horse.
speaker: narrator

text: It is a sturdy iron rake with a wooden handle.
speaker: narrator

text: "You must go see my daughter, Hilde, who sells our fruits and vegetables on Market Street."
speaker: heinrich

text: Go ahead.  Ask him something.
speaker: narrator

text: "I am Heinrich Pferdefedern.  I live in town with my daughter Hilde, who sells our produce on Market Street."
speaker: heinrich

text: I'm sorry.  I know little about the Baron and his family.
speaker: unsure-heinrich

text: "Hilde is a bright little filly.  I'm very proud of her."
speaker: heinrich

text: "We Centaurs are a very ancient and proud people."
speaker: heinrich

text: "It is truly a sight to see."
speaker: heinrich

text: "There are a few carrots and potatoes left from last season, but I'm just starting to plant this year's crops."
speaker: heinrich

text: "I can grow enough on this little plot to feed my family and the village.  You should see this field at harvest time!"
speaker: heinrich

text: "The brigands attacked me a few months ago.  I am a strong fighter and my hooves are deadly, but there were too many of them."
speaker: heinrich

text: "They nearly killed me.  My right leg was broken, and there is no pride for a Centaur who cannot run."
speaker: heinrich

text: "If it had not been for the unlikely intervention of their leader, I would be dead."
speaker: heinrich

text: "The leader came up while the brigands attacked me and forced them to stop, ordering them not to hurt people from the town."
speaker: heinrich

text: "Then, to my amazement, the leader gave me a Healing Potion and had me carried to the Healer's house."
speaker: heinrich

text: "It was hard to judge the leader, whose face was obscured by a large helm, but there was compassion unlike a brigand."
speaker: heinrich

text: "The Healer lives just east of here.  She is very skilled with potions."
speaker: heinrich

text: "The late snow has made the mountains very pretty.  Fortunately, our valley is sheltered, so the frost has not come to my fields."
speaker: heinrich

text: "Fields must be carefully tended to produce the best crops."
speaker: heinrich

text: "Our stand is in the northeast corner of Spielburg.  The produce is not of the finest this time of year, but you will not find better than Pferdefedern fruit come harvest time."
speaker: heinrich

text: We grow fine fruit in our little orchard, and sell it at our stand in town.  There you will find fresh cherries in the spring, peaches in summer, and apples in the fall."
speaker: heinrich

text: Climbing this wall is too difficult for your level of skill.  Keep practicing.

text: The meadow lies covered with a blanket of flowers, unusual for this early in the spring.  It is warm, even though surrounded by the late snows of winter.

text: The air has the fresh, clean scent of the mountains, accompanied by numerous perfume-like fragrances.

text: The "Trigger" spell is used to set off an existing spell, just like pulling the spell's "trigger".
speaker: erasmus

text: For example, if a chest has a magical trap on it, you can set off the trap by casting a "Trigger" spell.
speaker: erasmus

text: Just make sure you're out of range of the trap.
speaker: erasmus

text: I can set up other spells and let someone else "Trigger" them.
speaker: erasmus

text: I fixed Henry the Hermit's cave for him so that he can do alot of magic with only one spell.
speaker: erasmus

text: Now that's a spell with charm!
speaker: fenrus

text: It's true name is Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle, by the way.
speaker: erasmus

text: You cast the spell and anyone looking directly at its corruscating light is blinded for awhile and can't do anything.
speaker: erasmus

text: Magical traps are a good way to keep thieves out of your chests and other places.
speaker: erasmus

text: Put a fire trap, for example, on a chest.  When someone tries to open it, the thief will go up in smoke!
speaker: erasmus

text: Tell our hero here what happens when you forget and try to open the chest yourself.

text: The game is called Dag-Nab-It.  You take turns with the Chief Thief throwing daggers at the board.  Each player gets three turns, and throws three daggers in each turn.

text: More points are scored the closer each dagger hits to the center of the board.  Highest score wins the bet.
speaker: narrator

text: Adjust force and angle of throw with the arrow keys or mouse, according to your hand position.  Press Enter or click on the small dag-nab-it board to throw.
